Released: 26 March 2026

This is a patch release of PGD 5, which includes several enhancements and bug fixes. 

## Enhancements

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Version</th><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Improved the group deletion process to automatically drop associated replication sets.</summary><hr/><p>Enhanced the node group removal logic to automatically drop replication sets of the same name.</p>
</details></td><td>57531</td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Improved the consistency of Connection Manager against <code>pg_hba.conf</code>.</summary><hr/><p>Enhanced how the Connection Manager matches database names and usernames against <code>pg_hba.conf</code> fields. Key improvements include support for <code>sameuser</code> matching, refined <code>samerole</code> logic, and expanded <code>+rolename</code> matching to include the role itself and indirect members. Additionally, quotes are now stripped from usernames before matching to ensure consistent authentication behavior.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Added support for user name mapping (<code>pg_ident.conf</code>) to the Connection Manager.</summary><hr/><p>This enhancement allows the Connection Manager to use <code>pg_ident.conf</code> for user name mapping. Currently, this functionality applies to the <code>cert</code> authentication method, aligning Connection Manager behavior with PostgreSQL’s native authentication identity mapping.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Bug Fixes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Version</th><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Fixed a replication crash loop caused by interrupted partition detaching.</summary><hr/><p>Resolved an issue where logical replication would enter an infinite crash loop if a <code>DETACH PARTITION CONCURRENTLY</code> command was interrupted. Previously, the walsender would fail with the error <code>relation has no parent because it's being detached</code> when decoding WAL for a partition in a <code>detach-pending</code> state.</p>
</details></td><td>53628, 59291</td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Improved lock handling during change application to prevent data corruption from concurrent backends.</summary><hr/><p>Table and index locks are now held until transaction commit when applying changes. This ensures correct serialization and prevents data consistency issues during concurrent operations, specifically addressing risks introduced by parallel apply.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Parallel Apply can now be enabled when PGD is deployed with community Postgres</summary><hr/><p>Resolved an issue where enabling Parallel Apply on community PostgreSQL caused writer hangs and stuck subscriptions due to frequent lock timeouts. This fix allows Parallel Apply to be used reliably without requiring it to be disabled as a workaround.</p>
</details></td><td>54565, 57005, 57327</td></tr>
<tr><td>PGD CLI</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Fixed the in-line help for <code>pgd group set-option</code> command.</summary><hr/><p>Corrected the help text for <code>pgd group set-option</code> to accurately reflect group-level options.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>PGD CLI</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Fixed the matrix view output for the <code>pgd replication show</code> command.</summary><hr/><p>Inactive/disconnected slots now display <code>!</code> instead of <code>*</code> when lag is below 10 MB, restoring consistency.</p>
</details></td><td>56295</td></tr>
<tr><td>PGD CLI</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Backported TDE options for the <code>pgd node upgrade</code> command to PGD 5.</summary><hr/><p>The <code>pgd node upgrade</code> command now supports the <code>in-place</code> conversion of a non-TDE PGD node to a TDE-enabled node via the <code>--key-unwrap-command</code> and <code>--copy-by-block</code> options.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Fixed a memory leak in the consensus process when subscriber-only nodes are down.</summary><hr/><p>Resolved a malloc-based memory leak related to the improper freeing of connection strings.</p>
</details></td><td>57094</td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Fixed a segmentation fault occurring during <code>COPY</code> commands when <code>pgaudit.role</code> set to a non-default value.</summary><hr/><p>Resolved an issue where Postgres would crash with a segmentation fault when executing a <code>COPY</code> command if the <code>pgaudit.role</code> configuration was set to a non-default value.</p>
</details></td><td>58060</td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue where the <code>use_https</code> node group configuration option was ignored.</summary><hr/><p>Resolved a bug where Connection Manager incorrectly ignored the <code>use_https</code> setting. When upgrading to PGD 6.3.0 or 5.9.2, you must explicitly enable HTTPS by setting the <code>use_https</code> group option to <code>true</code> (in addition to having <code>ssl_cert_file</code> and <code>ssl_key_file</code> configured in <code>postgresql.conf</code> or via the command line) before the upgrade to maintain previous behavior.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.2</td><td><details><summary>Fixed a Connection Manager memory leak occurring during configuration reloads.</summary><hr/><p>Resolved a memory leak in the Connection Manager caused by incorrect handling of Postgres memory contexts during configuration reloads.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
